channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition — 142 replies — 568.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/BBBY/ - Larry Cheng went onto Theppshow edition

Open thread • Replies: 142 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:55 UTC

OP

>rc buys 7,780,000 shares of BBBY, and $60-$80 strike calls worth 1,670,100 shares in March 2022

>rc sells shares and calls of BBBY in august 2022

>rc has a sitdown with a famous corporate raider, Carl Icahn in october 2022

>rc offers 400 million for BBBY in December 2022

>pitchbook (best M&A software suite in the world, costing 5 figures + per year) lists BBBY(now known as BBBYQ or DK-Butterfly-1) as sold via a leveraged buyout on January 13 2023

>a default on the ABL with JPM happened on the same day that pitchbook shows a leveraged buyout, the terms of that ABL specify that a default is triggered if a change of control happens, a change of control is defined as an acquisition of 40% or more of the voting power by any person or group.

>one month later, February 2023 RC tweets “someone tell the government to stop shooting down my balloons”, alluding to bad actors delaying the Bed Bath m&a

>the GameStop CEO then became a BBBYQ chapter 11 creditor and debtor

>Larry cheng (who helped RC build Chewy, and is a current boardmember at GameStop, and a close personal friend to RC) went onto theppshow podcast (which is dedicated to BBBY recovery theories) 8 months after BBBY filed for chapter 11, and 2 months after shares were cancelled

>OverStock - who is the most famous company to battle illegal naked shorts, who even released a crypto dividend 5 years ago - purchased the BBBY IP, and owns 55% of TZero - the only approved blockchain entity who can issue debt instruments and securities

>OverStock - whos now traded under ticker BBBY - has issued warrants with dates that match GME's warrants, and with a CUSIP-6 that matches BBBYQ's CUSIP-6, proving BBBYQ has already begun m&a activity with BBBY


REQUIRED READING: bbbwhy.com

>Larry Cheng and Bill Pulte have gone onto theppshow podcast

jake2b started a youtube channel to access quality BBBYQ easier than scrolling twitter posts:

https://www.youtube.com/@jake2b

previous thread:
>>62417410

Early replies (first 3)

62438274
>Larry Cheng was inside the PP show

62438733
Good morning my beautiful Towelkings.

62438734
uh... guys... you might want to check your accounts...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62446088
>>62445569

62446106
>>62445576
>>62445600
>>62445645

spot on

62446149
>>62446088
They leave trading open so smart people can sell "for tax purposes" and get the loss on their tax return. I guess they think idiots buying 7 biĺion worthless securities for 8 cents each is funny,,,,,i do

62446238
>>62445569
what is this image even from?

62446242
>>62446238
wait I found it:

https://www.timesofisrael.com/us-hockey-team-owner-suspended-after-social-media-spat-with-fan-over-gaza-war/

HOLY SHIT ITS FROM 2024. Imagine what him seething at RC has done to him since?

62446288
>>62446242
Hes 60 years old whats the joke here?

62446344
>>62446242
<suspended


Lmao so nothing happened to him except a meaningless press release BBBYbbqers cant stop losing

62446386
>>62446344
the real fun starts when the rico roundup is in full swing. But even better is when cash and new equity is distributed to previous BBBYQ holders.

Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

62446441
>>62446386
>Guess what happens when tzero gets involved.

Your $0 stays $0

62447283
CUSIP-6

62447659
Ryan Cohen confirmed AGAIN that bathroom gods have won by posting on X that shows he has ties to the Icahn family. Shills will immediately come out of the woodwork now that I bring this up. It's over shills, we won. It's been 100% confirmed.

62447686
>>62447659
it's not brett icahn's fiance the birthmarks don't match

62447756
Baggie general

62447918
>>62447756

62448131
lamestop holders are seething because they missed out on the true moass. bathroom gods are the chosen ones. they trusted the plan above all. the cancellation of shares was the ultimate IQ test. weaker people couldn't handle that. now they are filled with regret.

62448146
>>62448131
The silo around BBBY was truly impressive. Superstonk made no mention of it. /GME/ was a full blown silo as well. It wasn't until I looked at the option chain on BBBY and saw the insane volatility that made me look in to it further. Greatest decision of my life.

62449186
>>62449050
all of them are short bbbyq. i guarantee it

62449344
>>62449050
>>62449186
If you are assassinated, does your short position get closed? Asking for Brian Thompson

62449448
>>62449344
Nope. His estate would get liquidated to pay off to buy back the shares.

62450370
CUSIP-6

62450461
>>62448146
The silo around eBay has become palpable

62452261
she has high res photos on her facebook. you can compare the birthmarks on her arm. the redditors are stupid as fuck it's demoralizing.

62452270
it seems that sultan didn't tweet about teddy like i thought he did. fuck's sake.

62452287
>>62452270
Its right here can you stop whining like a fatherless attention starved faggot? Youre scaring the hoes.

https://x.com/Almaadeed/status/2072817361862717939

62452325
>>62452287
ok good it displays the gamestop.com preview on the twitter page but the link he posted is to teddy.com which redirects to gamestop.com

62452826
>>62450461
The silo around BBBYQ has become Papaliable

62454181
CUSIP-6

62454621
>>62454181
baste

item.title: I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. — 86 replies — 114.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job.

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:25 UTC

OP

I've been a NEET for a long time and I'm finally trying to get a job. I'm not young, 40 next year, and don't have a job history or references. High school graduate.
How fucked am I?

Early replies (first 3)

62449979
I live in California, too.

62449982
What did you do for the last 23 years? Hard to help without more context. Do you have skills?

62449989
>>62449982
Depression and NEET means not in education employment or training.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451728
>>62451705
You're in California, apply for every single possible free service they have and physically go into a government center to get someone to set you up. Just spam apply to absolutely everything and make up Mcdonald's tier work history since they'll almost never call references (even better if you have a friend to lie for you though). Aggressively save every penny when you get a job and you'll be alright. You need to try to force yourself to be as optimistic as possible during the entire process and be comfortable with stretching the truth or coming up with a story about what you were doing, everyone does this.

62451729
>>62451718
Great advice, some of these positions include a dental plan I believe

62451733
>>62451705
Which I just looked up and could cost thousands of dollars. Medical night not cover it either.
>>62451718
The reason no one wants that job is because it sucks. Have you ever changed an adults diapers? I would rather eat buckshot then do that again. Besides, I don't think it pays more than a grocery store cashier anyway.

62451737
>>62451733
You've already done it and have experience in it, honestly this seems like the natural way for you, it's like life has been preparing you to deal with shit, adult shit

62451741
>>62451729
Oh good God, are you serious? I can't do the job anyway.

62451742
>>62451539
People who have worked their whole lives at least have learned HOW to work and have a resume to prove it. But that is really only the bare minimum. There is much more to a fulfilling life, much of which has nothing to do with work or money, but still arises out of self-discipline, skill-development, and emotionally maturity.

62451745
>>62451705
Dentalfag reporting in.
If you post some pics I can tell you which ones could be saved with a crown and which you should have extracted ASAP

62451748
>>62451705
Forgot to add you need to fix your teeth by any means necessary, even if it means ripping that shit out of your head because if you don't it will kill you slowly and painfully by infection. Just aggressively pursue free state services.

62451816
I like these threads, it makes me feel better about my own life. But OP seriously how did you get to this point? You would think alarm bells would be going off in your head at age 25, 30 etc. that you need to get your shit together, for fuck sakes your parents aren't going to live forever.

62451821
>>62451099
It's thievery
He can't admit to crimes nigga
You dense

62451832
>>62451403
Wow shouldn't have cut internet mom lol

62451837
Healthcare or zogbot
Neither a comfy choice, but assuming healthcare, you can start sweeping floors then move up to some sort of auxiliary nurse, nursing assistant, then nurse eventually. You could reach 6 figs, but the path would take a fair amount of humiliation, wiping floors and boomer feces

62451874
>>62451745
This anon is offering something genuinely good for seemingly free.
I'm just noting that

62452257
>>62451063
OP check out this guy sno0ks on YouTube, he has just gone through a similar life experience to you and has vlogged about it: https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=VQsdy_yaQAQ&ra=m

62452288
Go to school
Get work experience
Get a real job
Jesus Christ most people figure this out when they’re 18 years old you god damn retard.

62452842
>>62451745
There's nothing to save, I have two teeth that are broken off at the gum line and the gums have grown over them, another tooth lost the back half above the gum line and my lower right molar is broken on top and has a chunk missing.
They all have to come out.

62452847
>>62452288
What school? I'm already a high school graduate.
>>62451821
Food stamps, dum dum.

62453356
What about trying to start a business? Self employment? Why do my only options have to be depressing bullshit?

62453476
>>62453356

62453498
>>62453356
Both are better (best?) options for wealth if you don't plan on going to college and also marrying someone who went to school. Both preferably masters degrees or higher.

62453510
>>62449961
I have an online friend who was a loser NEET for like 10 years, he's still a loser but he started editing videos for youtube channels for a % of revenue and one of them popped off and he's made around 220k in 2 years. If you are autistic about something and use the internet, it's possible, though you have to get lucky and work hard still

62453537
>>62453356
You need some capital to get started, whatever biz you want to open. And since you sound incredibly lazy and your body most likely haven't a single gram of muscular mass, you probably wont do anything outside, correct?
Then you need to make cash from your computer.

I started scalping and flipping TCGs back in 2016 with $4K invested. Slow start but it eventually paid off after a bunch of smart picks on my end (no sealed, i focused on singles at release i thought could become staples, extremely risky and you need to be very good at the game itself to correctly identify unnoticed bangers). Once i secured more cash i could start to focus on vintage staples and that's where the $$ are. Cashed out 100K total so it turned out pretty nicely.

If you have zero you can try to make AI pornslop, twitch content or stuff like that. But starting from scratches, with zero visibility, zero social skill and no determination in a field ultra-saturated with mega grinders who want to succeed... i don't think you'll go very far. Not to discourage you, but what you have showed us of your personality so far tells me you most likely will end up as a beggar living under a bridge.

62453557
>>62453510
Video editing is a shitload of work hours, if you reads OP shitpost you'll quickly understand it's out of his reach. He's basically asking if we can give him a winning lottery ticket, and when we tell him it doesn't work that way, he whines.

62453686
>>62453557
You would need a computer for that and an Internet connection. The desktop unused to have died several years ago and I'm only allowed to use the wifi with my phone.
I would have to move out completely first.

62453688
Also, if you guys could calm down on the personal attacks? I didn't do anything to deserve that shit. Whatever boggeyman lives in your head isn't me.

62453723
God, it feels like I'm having panic attack after panic attack.

62453813
>>62453723
honest to god you should go work on a boat/ship, get paid to roam the seas and hear stuff on the radio and sleep in the middle of the darkness of the ocean and occasionally see whales.

62453981
>>62449961
This is why I tell my neet friend to grt out and go grab a job, even packing groceries before you reach 40 something. YOU WILL REGRET NEETING

62454510
seconding personal home aide/caregiver
gotta get your shoe in the door somehow

https://www.uscareerinstitute.edu/online-caregiver-school
tuition: $485
2-4 months online part time training

62454560
Become a trans prostitute

item.title: House buying — 79 replies — 112.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
House buying

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:28 UTC

OP

I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.

What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.

Early replies (first 3)

62447251
>>62447246
>every normie I meet says
You could buy and then the market severely crashes.
Housing shouldn't be a financial asset but that's corporatism for you.

62447257
>>62447251
Yea i guess but the stock market "could" also crash and i lose everything. That's not really insightful advice.

62447265
Lmao you are a rentoid

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448311
>>62447843
>Just shut up and buy my bags, goy

62448426
For $180k you could buy 5 acres of rural land and build at least the envelope of a house and not owe anyone anything.

62448439
>>62447246
do nigresses really goon to this kind of slop? Do they even read?

62448441
>>62448426
$180k would barely get you a livable house in Eastern Europe today.

62448442
>>62447246
>bwwm

>italians
>white

62448454
>>62447823
>fast forward 20 years
wont even take a third of that timeline, 38% of the boomer generation already croaked, look it up if you don't believe me.
We haven't seen much of an impact on the prices yet (well, depends where you live becuz here it already tanked a good 25% from 2022 highs) because it's younger boomies inheriting all the houses from older boomers. And since they are boomers, they know what they got and have enough cash reserves to sit on empty houses for years, waiting for a bounce back who will never come. I know becuz my parents inherited 3 houses the past 5 years and sold only one lol.

62448456
>>62448442
the dude literally is darker than the semi-bleached muttess lol. What is going on in BW heads?

62448596
>>62447246
>because housing will only get more expensive.
Maybe, likely so for big cities that get more job offerings and infinite jeets/immigrants. Rural areas? I am not sure, those might start to drop once boomers die off and zoomers/alphas are too poor and low in numbers and not interested in rural houses.

62448620
>>62448596
Incel rentoids now experts on the housing market, is there anything they dont know?

62448624
>>62448620
I am a proud owner of a commieblock apartment and some fields and forestland that I got from my parents/grandparents.

62448648
>>62448441
Build from scratch be your own contractor

62448659
>>62448624
Thats nice maybe keep out of discussions on America if from commieblock land

62448682
>>62448659
>implying you're in the US
idiot. If we kick the foreigners out you'll be gone first.

62448689
Normies are not getting mortgages because rates are like 6.5% when they would not normally be 3%>.

62448696
>>62448689
Boomers paid 20%

62448701
>>62447286
>2nd house for 425k around 31.
>2nd house is worth 900k now
What happened is their dollars lost half the purchasing power.
BTW they didn't but the house for $425k. They bought it for that plus whatever they pay in interest over time. Which is very likely to be closer to the amount they sold it for later.
It's nice to build equity, but don't be a fool.

62449002
>>62447246
>I have $180k sitting in an S&P500 index fund and every normie I meet says I'm making a mistake by not getting a mortgage ASAP because housing will only get more expensive.
>What is the /biz/ philosophy on this advice? I'm a rentoid and currently pay only around $1000 a month.
I'm in a similar position to you and unless you have a lifestyle reason to need a house it doesn't make financial sense right now IMO. Look at it financially, houses cost probably on the ballpark of 300-350k, let's just assume 325k median in a low to medium cost area, that's a 145k loan, at 6.25% interest rate the annual loss of interest only of 9k on year 1 and 8.9k on year 2 contrasted with an annual net loss currently of 12k a year renting but you also dont have other costs like property taxes, insurance, and maintenance costs that should be baked into rent but are not going to baked into that interest loss of yhe house so add those in too, where i live I'd pay 6k prop tax, 1k insurance, and rule of thumb is to budget 1-3% of home value so 3.25k-9.8k. To build on the case if you have money in the SP500 it has returned 10.5% annually (18.9k) or if you want to play it safe 4.5% in CDs (8.1k). That said, it depends on how long you are looking to buy a house, how risk tolerant you are, and if your landlord wants to increase rents. Run those numbers down a spreadsheet and see what makes sense for you in your area but i have come to the conclusion of letting my money ride in the market for the time being knowing the risk that I could get burned.

62450291
>>62447246
my advice is quit snooping around your mom's secret book collection

62450516
average home price prior to covid was 4.5 years of salary; now its closer to 6.8 years of salary. prices are too high and is way cheaper to rent for now. i save while renting to buy outright, fuck a mortgage.

62450518
>>62448701
>nice to build equity
>depreciating asset requires upkeep
>no where to go but up

ive seen this movie before, sometime around 2008 i think

62450563
>>62450516
Thats a you problem if you cant double your wage post covid

62450591
I know this is biz but everyone here is talking about buying a house as though you have to make money off the decision, I could care less about making money off my house, I bought it because it was a huge lifestyle upgrade and now I live 100x better than when I was renting an apartment, it feels like I finally started my real life.

62450594
>>62447246
Let the boomers deal in the real world. Housing is a terrible investment if there is any risk to your income over the term of the loan. My cousin sold TSLA to put a down-payment on a home $120k. The most money he ever had. Home price was $230k. Turned out the home was a lemon and he had dumped another $80k into repairs. Guess what? He lost the house when he and his wife's winery side hustle failed. $200k given to bank for a couple years being a homeowner. Had he put the money into an index fund he'd be worth half a million by now.

62450595
houses are for living in, not speculation

62450600
>>62450594
the math on this story isn't adding up, bro had a $110K loan after that down payment which is only like $500 a month, sounds like he was just bad at finance if he lost it with that payment

62450606
>>62448058
Jealous blk boi

62450608
>>62450600
Rentoids all suck at math their anecdotes are fake as fuck but real in their bitter minds

62450897
My house is paid off. Feels great man. 6 bedrooms 3.5 baths on half an acre. Great home for my family of 6.

62451180
>>62450600
You can't even follow basic math. He had a $190k loan at around 6%. Hardly $500 a month with insurance, taxes, mortgage insurance, etc.

>>62450608
My cost to rent is 11% of my take home pay. You are spending more on a mortgage with no guarantee of keeping your home.

Biz really has the dumbest anons.

62454567
>>62447246
I unironically read this book

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 114 replies — 102.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 114 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:03 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62442507
>>62442214
Yup, little by little.

62442511
>>62442507
>>62442228
I dollar cost. THe past few months have been gr8

62442553
>>62442511
Where do you do that

62442563
>>62426512
I am DCAing in

62442650
>>62442503
memecoins pump faster. I made money on Ansem and some Solana gems

62442655
>>62439758
never trust centralised services

62442658
buy btc goys

62442668
>>62442658
oy vey

62443383
>>62442650
Well, that's good for you.
>>62442655
Same. Self-custody first, then I check if the protocol has had multiple audits.

62443384
>>62442658
Do it man!

62443930
>>62442668
Goy

62444179
I'm waiting for October, but if it goes below 55k I'll probably start slurping. Everyone who says crypto I'd dead is retarded. Not ripping = dead is their logic.

62444587
>>62426537
That's never going to happen, max will be around 45K. My plan is to DCA, I got in around 58K, stake a few on babylon, I'm waiting to see if there will be more dump

62444608
>>62444587
You really trust the Babylon protocol? It's my first time looking into it and I see I could earn 0.02+ BTC every month. $1200+ every month sounds much better than sitting in my Ledger.

62444998
>>62444608
really

62445005
>>62426512
I'm selling at 100k. I've had enough of holding and should have sold last year. I wanna get out of this shit, it's too much stress.

62445018
>>62427357
do not pull

62445689
>>62444998
DYOR

62446816
>>62445018
Who's plebs rn?

62447133
>>62445005
regrets?

62447140
Ngmi

62447522
>>62442255
Just keep going

62448616
>>62426537
this is probably correct : (

trump and the political class already made their money off crypto last couple years

62448797
>>62427359
Surely the bottom would be zero, no?

62449904
>>62448797
people like to imagine when theyre on drugs

62450375
Why

62450895
Buy Bitcoin

62450912
>>62426512
Buy mote bitcoin and wait for next halving to dump on the normies.

62453141
>>62444608
The idea is to make BTC work even while holding just like ETH instead of leaving it idle, and I like the idea of self custody on that protocol

62454511
>>62426638
this but unironically

item.title: Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/? — 58 replies — 86.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

Open thread • Replies: 58 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:30 UTC

OP

Any active duty US military members or veterans on /biz/?

I joined the military and I wanna know how to get a million dollars. I put 10% of my income into my retirement account and the rest of my free cash into index funds.

Early replies (first 3)

62447635
>>62447595
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sZZaOR4UCnE

62447641
>>62447595
The only smart play is to aim for a 100% disability rating which you can get in ~1 to 4 years (1 enlistment).
I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life.
I am a vet and I get nothing.

62447664
>>62447595
If you're not high rank or close to information in some other way then it's just another job. Not gonna change much in terms of how getting a million would look like, you'll have to gamble like the rest of us.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62448525
>>62448503
>>62448482
Why don't you go recruit your fellow circumcised niggers, we could all do without them lmao

62448554
>>62448525
Mental illness

62448558
>>62448554
Lol disabled keyboard warrior downy

62448572
>>62447635
>>62447740

If any of you actually served you would know how anti-Israel the military is.

62448579
>>62448572
Which military, the iranian military

As for the US just keep sucking those circumcised peckers, they own you, bitch all you want about it

62448581
>>62447641
> I know several guys getting $4000+ a month for life
What a waste of $4000 going to the lower classes.

62448588
>>62448251
Just reply to them once. No need to point out why they glow. Dumb ass.

62448608
>>62447962
>>62448482
>>62448503
>>62448554
OCPD-poster detected. Go praise the jews elsewhere.

62448679
Last drone video about why doing war in the drone age is utter shit. https://i.4cdn.org/k/1783172321354125.webm

62448903
>>62448588
I just want it to stop. None of them care that this shit is extra inauthentic and borderline schizoid.

62448986
>>62448469
>>62448452
And this shit too
Some 29yo bitch talking about how young men should be sent to a meat grinder while her husband is 65.

Literally boomers trying to kill zoomers so they can fuck their daughters.

62449000
>>62447724
>promote it
You're the one promoting the idea of invading iran for real, genius, I'm telling you if you think it's "a better idea' then you can go hang out with the glowcrew.

62449029
>>62448022
Navy is a good way to save but realistically Coast Guard is probably nicer. Have a plan on saving $50k in 3yrs without deployment then put it in a fund and watch it grow

>>62448254
If they're going to be gay retards sure, but something harder to prove like mental health is easier.
VAC gets denied because you need consistent med appts when you're in otherwise you get nothing

62449133
>>62448581
The military is a welfare system for the power classes.

62449315
>>62447961
This is so true it's not even funny. I also can't emphasize enough how many retards BURN MONEY on new cars while laughing at me for driving an 07 shitbox.
I have never seen so many $50-80k cars in my life, trucks, SUVs and even a brand new Tesla. After a bit of time in you'll n/o/tice

62449322
>>62447595
the only way for the military members to make money is to go rob other country and sell drugs nigger
thats what the ships are for nigger

62449330
>>62447595
Unironically document your injuries, apply for appropriate disability before getting out

then start applying for fed jobs immediately. Use your college money. Hire on with the government. In time you might try government contracting, it pays a lot more than waging. But even fed wagies are often millionaires. Buy a house, that helps. If you work for the government they'll help you buy a house and then sell it if you move.

62449744
>Let the goyim deal in the real world

62450947
>>62448572
>reddit spacing
>post written by a zogbot with an israeli flag within 100ft at all times
>erm actually the heckin military hates its sole mission of fighting wars for israel

62451074
>>62447697
Israel/US jews did 9/11.
>t. two deployment Army veteran

62451135
>>62448022
They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once. Which is virtually everyone. The only way to get what is owed now is to apply for disability. When everyone who got out in the last 20 years is telling you to do it, you do that. Do not trust anyone in active service about this!

I wish I saved the shifty eyed army recruiter Wojack.

62451175
>>62451074
>two deployment Army Veteran
at least you admit you're a retard

62451198
>>62451135
Seriously do not trust them. Soldiers have a difficult adjustment to civilian life because they have to switch to from blind order following to street smarts where even their friends can back stab them. Soldiers who never adopt the either get killed with their deaths covered up. Sometimes soldiers just get killed for no reason.

62451215
>>62448579
>>62450947
youre so mindbroken lmao

62451282
>>62451215
You will never be a patriot

62451292
>>62447641
This. From everything I've heard and seen, aiming for disability is the way to go.

You will probably lose your dignity in the pursuit of this though, but the long-term benefits are probably worth it. They might be clamping down on this under Hegseth I imagine, but maybe it depends on your branch.

62451309
>>62447797
Its a good idea because you actually GET something not just numbers on a screen.
He might have $1 Million to pay down later but he has atleast 4 properties providing him passive income.
Not just income to pay down the mortgages, but actually passive income on top of that.
Landlording is extremely profitable and if you don't understand it you shouldn't get into it.
But you are fucking way the fuck up by not using the VA loan ever. It's an instant ticket to real estate. Buy with the intention to landlord and you will go far.
>>62451135
>>62451198
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once
You don't live in reality.
There are millions of vets recieving disability. The point of disability is so the government doesnt get sued. It's workman's comp.
If what you said was true then no one would recieve it.
You probably dont even know the types of discharges that qualify you for VA disability or P&T vs TDIU. You don't know none of the details of course.
You don't care because you just want to schizopost.

This is why you dont trust stupid old people on this site. They just think they know it all and get more stubborn and post borderline schizo shit.

62451317
>>62451292
VA is for every branch.
No one is taking away VA benefits the government would be sued.
Hegseth doesnt matter.
VA benefits = Workers comp
That is the entire point of it existing.

62451419
>>62451135
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once.
KEK. I just realized you know even less than I thought initially.
You do know the VA doesnt give disability until after you are out of the service, right?
How can they take away your bennies when you have an honorable discharge?
You probably don't even know what types of discharges there are yet you're saying this shit.
>>62451198
Is this what happened to your dad?
Failed to switch between blindly following orders and street smarts? And died?
>>62447896
>My family quit being a military family
>because of negations of benefits
>They negate all benefits if you show the slightest hesitation at following orders even once
What the fuck does this mean? Your dad was dishonorably discharged for disobeying orders? Just say that.
See this is why OP called you a schizophrenic incel.
I know Im replying way too much to these posts but this is not how reality works at all. Your view is not reality.

62454571
>>62447595
Vet here. AMA

item.title: BEARISH ON COSTCO — 74 replies — 81.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
BEARISH ON COSTCO

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:15 UTC

OP

People are scamming costcos lenient return policy now. Should we short COST?

Video:
https://x.com/Basedgoymemes/status/2073860759269077199

Early replies (first 3)

62449396
based

62449400
I wouldn't call jeets 'people'

62449441
Fuck this racist, post the person who started to do this was some white bitch, she did it for 12 years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62452229
>>62449388
Why would this be bearish? It increased their DEI score and that means they get infinite free loans from blackrock

62452396
>>62449441
Karen means victory

62452975
>>62452396
Cope

62453019
>>62449388
Indian mentality is completely toxic to civilization

62453036
>/biz/
>fundamentally misunderstanding how Costco works as a business
You could return every single thing you bought at Costco and they'd still be profitable. The entire point of the company is raising capital. They have 60 days to process and refund their distributors for the returns. In that period, the millions they receive in up-front purchases generates them more in investment value with than what all the returns would pay out for. The fact that /biz/ fails to see this is why all of you are poor wagie shitcoin scammers. The business model Costco operates on is highly faceted and multilayered. They make millions a month in renting warehouse shipping palette space alone. The model they use is literally refund-proof and they don't give a shit about you bringing stuff back. Look at their stock chart if you want proof.

62453062
>>62453036
>they can afford it so it's ok to steal
Indian jugaad psychology on display. When every citizen in a country is aligned with this philosophy, it fails.
When there is high trust, there is communal wealth.

62453196
No honour system can survive contact with Jeets

62453215
>>62451853
> list falls off hard and immediately into literally who territory
saaar why is the indian tech industry a joke that is being cannibalize by chatgpt saaar

62453293
>>62453196
>my country is being invaded reeeeee
>better cry on an incel blog 24/7/365 until i die

62453303
>>62451853
>You are jelly of our success
lmao

62453347
>>62453303
You have 20 posts seething about indians who are the richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year, yes you are jealous of us

62453353
>>62453347
>richest immigrants in America averaging $200k per year
Brapmeet thinks we don't know about his household income stats with ten jeets crammed into each domicile. KEK

62453370
>>62453353
Ten indian Google coders at $400,000 per year each stuffed in one house, in 5 years each one buys a $1 million house for cash.

While you seethe and cry on incel blogs that you deserve everything for free because you are white, lmao no wonder you are bitter

62453683
>>62449441
>Lives in a white country instead of his shithole
Go home Rajesh nobody likes you

62453692
>>62453683
Your master invited me in because you are dumb and lazy and im smart and hardworking

62453742
>>62453062
This is the most blatant
>Thing: Japan
I've ever seen in my life. As if all of rural north america doesn't do this as well.

62454011
Jugaad

62454019
I ended up canceling my membership because the Indian situation became intolerable to deal with. They refunded me my full $65. Thank you CostCo

62454027
>>62453353
Clown house.

62454038
>>62449441
Bloody bitch benchod!
Take it back!
I place a curse on your tribe, bitch!

62454126
>>62454038
SAAAAAAAARS

62454135
>>62454019
Indians have conquered your country and colonized your mind

62454143
>>62449769
Go back now or in a few years you will graduate to compost fertilizer. Not that you jeets can read the times.

62454145
>>62454019
costco lives or dies off its public relations, which is partly why they allow the kind of subhumans like OP's subject to carry on for a while before cancelling their membership to ensure they're not perceived poorly
But yeah, they're going to have to do something about the flood of shitskins using 10 incomes to a household to shop there and I don't think a dozen new locations each year will solve it

62454349
Is anyone shorting

62454398
>>62454145
Kek

62454476
just wait, in 2 more years costco will have a jeet ceo and instead of hotdogs youre gonna get currywurst

62454493
>>62454349
The richest cohort in America shopping there and white incels are shorting? Kektop!

62454541
>>62454493
Seethe

item.title: Could always be worse — 79 replies — 73.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Could always be worse

Open thread • Replies: 79 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:05 UTC

OP

You could be a German wagie.

>no more AC
>no more sick leave

These are the early signs of a collapsing country. Just wait until the food riots hit.

Early replies (first 3)

62438577
former Blackrock manager is chancellor now, imagine.

62438607
>>62438545
Do you have to send this shit to govt? What if the employer doesn’t give a shit is someone going to audit this?

62438687
>>62438607
If this is anything like Germany's neighbouring country that I live in then the doctor sends it to the government when he inputs it into the system, and then it goes to your employer automatically from there so he knows whether to honour it or not.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62441472
>>62441451
Yeah and AfD with it’s obvious russian backing is an enemy faction, no matter how much it claims to be against immigrants and wokeism.

62441478
>>62438545
but i am
and -40% ytd
and fat
and no gf

62441575
>>62441375
For decades we've had this system and everyone was happy. The problem is that the economy is failing because German is very reliant on cheap energy which we've chosen to avoid ever since the ukraine war. If we had pivoted hard into manufacturing renewables or simply having kids we'd be fine.

>>62441427
The reactors we had weren't very good. Nowadays everyone is pro-nuclear because they're thinking of reactor types that weren't even conceptualized 10 years ago. Our problems are that we were reliant on cheap energy which we didn't bother producing and that we have no kids.

62442429
>>62441424
>>62441472
Just fuck off you retard. My relatives even fought against Russia in ww2. I bet YOU are the one not German, or what's your religion I wonder? Let me guess "atheism with Jewish family"? See >>62438768
And don't forget the government is the one prioritizing foreign interests.

62442433
>>62441478
Oooofff at least it's Friday.

62442461
>>62438545
That’s why I let myself be fired, collected unemployment and fucked off. Retarded country full of retarded autism max robots.

62443834
>>62440944
It would be a shame if you coughed into your hands and touched as many public surfaces as possible on the way to the doctor's.

62443931
>>62438545

Both of these are inconceivable to the Australian mind.

62444279
>>62438687

62444281
>>62440631

62445477
>>62438545

62445676

62445684

62446070

62446094

62446167
>>62442429

62446771
>>62446167

62446847
>>62446771

62447049
>>62438770

62449791
>>62446847

62449805
Merkel destroyed Germany, merz is finishing it off he will start a war with Russia he knows he cant win, the goal being the extermination of all german men just like they did to Ukraine , buy long term puts on the German market they will 1000X

62449815
>>62438545
Kek this is cucked. It's not even the corporation demanding it which makes this even worse.
>you vill work more while the rapefugees live on gibs
Kek imagine living in Germany and having to prove you're ill.

I just remembered they now have rules to prevent you from leaving for a prolonged duration despite the draft not being manadatory lol, lmao even. Oh but only males btw, females exempt.

62450529
>>62446167

62450539
>>62438770

62450552

62450633

Merz is an afd agent. He won't start a war with russia.
He will destroy the CDU and then hand the country with a massive military over to the afd which will proceed to invade europe

62450642
>>62450633
Is this what liberals think would happen with the afd? Lol no wonder they are scared shitless

The afd is just another puppet, nothing will change, did any of the other european countries go up in flames after their so called nazi party got into power? Heck some are even worse off with shitskins than before

62450654

62450969

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2 — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #2

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:57 UTC

OP

Brothers,

We spent 20 days and 203 posts together in the last thread and we have yet to formulate a method that will enable us to sit at the table in the six figure club with the rest of kinsmen. We knew this wouldn’t be easy so let's not get dispassionate.

For my next proposal I will say we focus on developing a skill that will increase our value. If we all level up on our own it will only take longer. If we all level up by 2-3 points a month then cumulatively we will all have gotten stronger as a mass society. Perhaps one amongst us can elevate themselves and come back to teach the others!

How long shall we be waiting then? How long will our own potential be mocked by others? To what limit will we continue to persist in the underclass?

Come, those who have not $10,000 to their name

Let us commence the general!

>Good /biz/ manga
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Good /biz/ movies
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Born_Rich_(2003_film)

>Good /biz/ books
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Gambler_(novel)

>Good /biz/ games
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Good /biz/ waifus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elizabeth_Holmes

>Agenda 2030 is on the way, have you studied what your enemy has planned out?
https://www.math.uwaterloo.ca/~ervrscay/TheGreatReset.pdf

Praeviously
>>62373752

Early replies (first 3)

62453729
All you faggots need to do is buy chainlink. Fuck your side hustles. You can get a suicide stack for 8k right now.

62453795
Last thread we spoke a lot on learning new skills but very few fruitful things arose. I realized the issue is that a lot of the proposed skills were just boring or too niche.

62453799
>>62453795
wait, are you asking poor people to discuss amongst themselves how to get rich?
kek

you don't learn how to get rich from poor people

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62453805
>>62453622

>you want six figures? Power wash 100 driveways for $100 each, there’s your six figures

62453846
>>62453799
Which came first, the poor or the rich?

62453864
>>62453846
Adam was rich. Then Eve the poor floozy showed up.

62454032
>>62453864
Blasphemy

62454046
>>62453622
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62454048
>>62453846
first there were only the strong and the weak, with the invention of property this evolved into the rich and poor.

62454058
i missed the last thread. anyway, what worked for me was a temp agency. worked for six months then walked away. back to NEETdom. i think it's best to learn new skills if you can find someone to pay you while you learn.

62454093
Best thread on /biz/ fuck the bezosposters and the ocpdfags

62454116
Some helpful posts from the last thread

62454170
I made 10k in the last 2 months from my waging

62454624
Bump

item.title: Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL… — 20 replies — 45.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment >Property taxes >Maintenance >Repairs because something WILL…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:44 UTC

OP

Why do people act like buying a house is a good investment
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
>Even if you're "beating inflation" with a mortgage you're paying much more than its actual cost
>Closing costs
>All just so you can sell it (likely at a loss when considering inflation) so you can buy a slightly less expensive house
It's not that buying a house is a bad purchase if you can afford it, but acting like it's somehow an "investment" is absolutely retarded, you'd probably make more back from buying Pokemon cards

Early replies (first 3)

62450381
>>62450377
You're supposed to buy houses on a discount
>t. Selling for double what I purchased in 2023 after $20K in upgrades LOL

62450391
>>62450377
Idk I just want one to live in, but maybe they rent it that way person who rents pays bills and when he break something you can tell him to pay for it and then when they don't give a fuck anymore they sell it

62450397
>cbf moving every year because the landlord decides they a wankers and want to sell to some Chinese immigrants who took our jobs

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450401
>>62450377

You really wanna know? For most people it’s because owning a house forces you to save via the mortgage payments. Yes, you could put that money into index funds instead or whatever and come out ahead, but normies wouldn’t do that, they’d spend it. For a single /biz/tard buying a house is probably not a good investment. But for most people, especially families, owning a house will improve their financial position over time because they will be forced to build equity over time instead of spending that money on stuff.

That’s why normies say housing is a “good investment”. For them it’s better than the likely alternative.

62450434
>>62450381
>double
you could've just bought MU, SNDK, etc.

62450436
It can work out cheaper than renting, depends on prices, now is probably a bad time to buy. No doubt all kinds of privilege taxes will be heaped on home owners too in the coming years, I think it's smarter to buy property outside of the west, the benefit also is that you can leave to live somewhere else at any time

62450445
Great, another rentoid cope thread

62450446
>>62450377
It’s hard to lose money from owning a house. What are the alternatives? Either rent where you get no money back, or if you’re lucky enough to live with parents who don’t ask for anything. Tax is a non issue. One year of tax is the equivalent of a renter paying one months rent. Maintenance is virtually cost free. Cleaning up the house is what any responsible human being does regardless of whether they own a house or not. Repairs happen anywhere. Learn to DIY. You shouldn’t need to spend $400 for someone to replace a $40 faucet that takes 30 minutes to do. It’s not only about investing from a financial point of view either. Owning a home is more piece of mind and being able to do whatever you want (not counting HOAs). That alone is worth it more than anything else

62450464
Renting when old will be a massive issue in years to come.
Pensions will not cover rent AND living for the vast majority of people.
It's an issue not reared its head yet because boomers own homes and have pensions that cover their living, or they are selling their homes for care.
Unless you know you are going to be wealthy, you need to own a home at some point or be utterly doomed to hardship in an already strained system in your old age.

>inb4 i'll just rope when old LULZ
Childish nonsense talk.

62451291
>>62450377
>Property taxes
>Maintenance
>Repairs because something WILL go wrong
all of this is wrapped up in your rent payment dumbass. you really think those costs don’t exist for your landlord and he’s not passing them on to you, plus profit?

62451450
buying a house cured my depression
best investment ever

62452006
when the market crashes everyone will still own their homes and will have enjoyed living in them but you'll own nothing and will have been living in an apartment listening to blacks keep you awake all night all that time

62452049
>>62450401
yea this tracks. it's like how a 401k is "good" in that it keeps retard normies from spending that chunk of their paycheck on coke and sportsball tickets

a good rule of thumb

62453524
>>62450377
I bought a 3-bedroom and rent out two of the bedrooms. My tenants pay my mortgage for me.
Housing was an "investment" for the last half-century or so, which is a big part of why things are so fucked now in the housing market.

Also, you're already paying for all that shit when you pay rent. You can either pay your rent to a bank directly or pay it to some retard who is skimming off the top. This realization is what led me to buy a house eventually.

62453548
An asset that can bring rent and only goes up in price. There is literally NO comparable investment. The only people crying about it are poor renters.

62453561
>>62450377
I don't like landlords telling me what I can and can't do with their property. I don't like having neighbors upstairs or downstairs. I don't like having to ask if I want something remodeled or changed. I don't like fighting with my neighbors for parking or who gets to use the yard or the laundry room.

also my house costs 1/5 what rent does because I've had it for 25 years and my payment never goes up while your rent has gone up 600% in the same time period.

62453579
>>62450377
You have to pay all that if you rent, except repair for damage that you didn't cause.

62454390
>>62450377
better than being a rentoid though

62454582
>>62454390
if you pay property taxes, you are a rentoid

62454589
>>62454582
>renter pays $48k to live in a house where I am
>I pay $4k in property taxes
somehow I don't think my $333 per month in "rent" is equal to your $4000 per month in rent

item.title: Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news? — 15 replies — 42.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Has Bitcoin become too dependent on ETFs and macro news?

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:49 UTC

OP

Seems like every BTC pump or dump is blamed on ETF flows or macro headlines.

>Is that just the reality now, or are people overlooking other factors?

Early replies (first 3)

62450753
>>62450728
Its 100% cz dependent, just tradingview has to write something to the price action.

62450760
>>62450728
Btc does this thing where it lulls the newfag into a false sense of superiority. Both in bull and bear markets. In a bull market, it tricks newfags into thinking it will go up forever and traps them in overleveraged longs and in bear markets, it tricks them into it never going back up and they either sell low, sell covered calls or lever up short and get milked. Just hold spot and don't pay attention for a decade.

62450902
>>62450753
cz?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62450909
>>62450760
In short = manipulation

62450916
>>62450902

62450937
>>62450916
Wait....what??

62450950
>thinks news drives the market

62450961
>>62450760
>just hold spot
>t. baggy
If you bought Bitcoin at the end of 2017 and held for 9 years, you would currently have no more value against gold than when you bought.

62450971
>>62450937
>not knowing who cz is

62451749
>>62450971
CZ is just another clown

62451999
>>62451749
A hardcore clown

62452810
>>62451999
the king of clown

62453044
>>62450961
I'm planning to buy silver. Is it good? Silver bar maybe

62454200
>>62450753
do you think the narrative actually influences sentiment after it's published?

62454605
>>62453044
Buy btc

item.title: Another coin that 10,000x that biz missed. — 13 replies — 38.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Another coin that 10,000x that biz missed.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:49 UTC

OP

Another coin that 10,000x that biz missed. I know meme coins are assed but god dam this shit is extreme humiliation while I hold shit like chainlink and ICP. All I had to do was buy $3k worth at the bottom and I’d had made $3 million dollars….

Early replies (first 3)

62447184
>memecoins in '26
yeah yeah yeah shut the fuck up

62447208
>>62447179
Ha. 259k here. Faggots. But, 99 PvP. Still, .01 in all would have made you money. But me? I’m just a simple eth holder 200x longer. Should I get an ad? Or an add? lol hippie. Younger a hair cut. And probably more, cause they do a batch deal where you can do a few or many hairs at the same time/ it’s like, some kind of buy og bit bean cut I’ve ever seen: so I’ve just taken to the stance of neglecting my portfolio just so I could live a little. Hurnlike. It gave the bears this false sense of security. Like I’m done buying n shirtszs. lol/ lmao even. Thanks for coming to my blog. Buy Everthing. And anything/ just buy.!

62447682
You can't pay me to buy something called literally BBC.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62447784
can you count? thats 1000x not 10000x .3 zeros

62447870
>>62447179

62448106
>>62447870
In ye olden days it would have just been posted on /biz/ along with DD, usecase and code.

62448726
already crashing btw

62448787
>>62447179
ICP to the moon

62449444
>>62448106
maybe a spicy whitepaper and a telegram that was popping with 5k members or so

62449502
It did a 1000x, not many people got a 10000x you muppet

62449603
>>62447179
completely fake numbers. every wallet controlled by same person

62451344
>>62448787
>jerryposting
Behold! The archetype of the typical ICP holder.

62454606
>>62447179
thanks just bought 100k

item.title: The Creampig Painpoint — 12 replies — 30.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Creampig Painpoint

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-07-07 10:46 UTC

OP

I'm trying to think of business models that will solve a simple set of painpoints: horny young men and poor young women. Basically, there are millions of young men with money who want to cream in a slampig (a creampig, if you will), and there are also millions of slampigs with no money, but all the obvious ways of letting young men pay for creampigs fall under prostitution, so the business model doesn't work in 90% of jurisdictions. Is there any way to make this work that ISN'T prostitution?

Early replies (first 3)

62449866
>>62449842
We get it anon. You like fatties. I do too. Not everybody wants to pay money to fill a hole.

62449884
>>62449842
It's called Tinder, and you're unfortunately late to the party on that one.

62449889
>>62449866
A lot of people do, though. Look at how big OnlyFans is, and that's all just cockteasing.

>>62449884
False. Tinder solved the painpoint of horny women who need to get fucked. Only a tiny percentage of men got anything out of it. It doesn't solve the horny men problem or the poor slampig problem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62449905
>>62449884
>and you're unfortunately late to the party on that one.
man you have no idea what you're talking about. what OP is talking about is curating a dating market that looksmatches and confines competition to a more equal standing or at least something more compatible. Tinder and every dating app has no incentive to do that, what they want to do and will always do until the end of time is enforce hypergamous and a competitive dating market but not excluding anyone and making them think they can score some random guy/girl that shows themselves in a picture and the "ooo maybe they'll heckin like me"; there's already fatigue and dating apps are best reserved for data mining or winners in hypergamy

62449932
>>62449905
Yes, well said. Maybe the trick is to have AI do a weighted looksmatch (women are given a slight advantage because they're pickier) and just assign each person three and only three options per week. Women are paid to use the site; men are not. If the woman doesn't pick an option, she doesn't get paid.

62449943
>>62449916
You know what? I like it, unironically. Very direct.

62450863
>>62449889
>>62449905
No you're both wrong and the other anon is correct about Tinder and other "matchmaking" apps. Men who want to secure matches badly enough will pay premium subscriptions for 5he chance to match. Then regardless of tier, everyone's advertising and behavioral profiling data is being sold in exchange for the opportunity to match with someone else. The meat market dynamic is in full play but instead of johns secretly meeting prostitutes 1:1 on a corner and haggling down on fiat price, its an open market where tracking data is sold to the pimp in exchange for bid access to the prostitute and fiat can be used to enhance bid count. Its an abstraction over the typical trade using advertising data as the main mode of exchange to avoid the legal issues that would come with running an actual prosititution ring, with the added benefit of the whores and johns both self assembling, the whores not realizing they're getting fucked on their cut of profit by matching with hundreds or thousands of people for free, and johns not realizing they're feeding the pimps free supply pricing information by doomscrolling/swiping.

62450990
>>62449932
Women not getting paid is probably the main selling point of the current structure. Its pure profit. Also they are picking options. They just all converge on the same 20% of guys, or happen to match with the guys who are willing to pay for premium. Capping at three options per week leaves money on the table.

I'm telling you, the current system is already an optimized version of what you're looking to build that uses advertising brokerage as a layer of abstraction which legally protects them from being considered as a prostitution racket.

The johns are already paypigging and the whores dont even realize they're getting shortchanged on a job they don't even realize they have because they're johning for Chad who's johnning for Stacy, and Stacy's the only one smart enough to just have her IRL pimp run her account to lure Chads into doing free labor by being a faceless performer in her next round OF content thanks to the hidden cams in the hotel room.

There's so many levels to this when you actually start paying attention its insane.

62451006
I want to creampie slampigs but I would not and cannot pay for it.

62451231
Invent DoorDash but with slampigs

62454586
>>62449842
this won't work retard

62454596
>>62449842
young men don't have money that's why young women like old men

item.title: Saylor sold 3,588 BTC — 59 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-07-07 08:41 UTC

OP

Saylor sold 3,588 BTC

Early replies (first 3)

62450542
such a comedy

buy high, sell low

mutts are cuckolds, france would conquer them in two weeks

62450550
>>62450537
Where Michael? Where?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62451234
>>62451185
You are useful in the sense that a crowd of your kind dominated the price action in the past few weeks, so even a very superficial reality check showed that Strategy was on sale, and you contributed to that discount.
I did load up those past two weeks, yes. Now that I'm done, I'm getting a little tired with these rumors, half-truths and other parroted garbage because I've read enough of it. I don't think of myself as particularly emotional, you can tell yourself that if it makes you feel better.

62451251
>>62450537
saylorcoin is FUCKED till this cuck is out

62451267
>>62450537
>unlimited dividends forever

62451294
>>62450537
He sold? Pump it

62451302
>>62451234
To the chair you go, cuck!

62451550
>>62451036
>>babby's first dead cat bounce
HAHAHA

62451591
saylor bought every top and now he is going to sell every bottom?

62451626
>>62450537
we goin 2 da mewn baby

62451629
>>62450537
https://x.com/Overdose_AI/status/2074139052563980439
What a shithead btw.

62451634
HE SODLED

62451659
>>62450537
Kinda funny that the dividend payments are 2x more than just taking out a loan on your bitcoin.

62452014
>>62451044
>Shitcoin seller says shitcoin needed, more at 8 pm!

62452040
>MicroStrategy's average purchase price for Bitcoin is approximately $75,476 per coin.

HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

62452054
>lose principle
>gain dividend
savvy investing

62452068
>buy high
>sell low

62452073
What do you call this strategy?

62452111
>>62451132
holy copium, i feel bad for you ngl

62452379
>>62451013
He bought those coins for less than 10k a coin.

Sure he will say it's an accounting loss on paper, but Saylor is ahead of the game.

62452424
>selling the bottom
He could have sold them for literally double last year top kek

62452435
>>62450537
This marks the bottom.

62452517
>>62450537
Aye so the price gotta go up to spite him lmao

62452682
>>62450537
I do believe that the market makers realized is more profitable to scam saylor another cycle into buy high sell low than to liquidate him, thus marking the end of the bear market and starting the upward crabbing rollercoaster until halvening which ofc will be frontrunned

62452752
Where is the liquidity for bidding up bitcoin going to come from in a world where AI eats all the capital, and retail investors are scared of losing their jobs

62452761
He is a genius, imagine collecting 800k btc with other people's money and all you have to do is top blast and sell the pico bottom

62452780
Nobody wants the Epstein pedo coin with shady Saylor, shady tether, shady creator…this is hot radioactive garbage

62452823
>>62452517
already back at $64k
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
he could've just waited 1 day and would sell at same price as he bought

62453016
>>62450537
This fucking maricon broke both of Frank Lopez’s rules:
>#1. Don’t ever estimate the OTHER guy’s greed!
As soon as anybody pumps bitcoin, everybody’s either taking profits or leveraging it lol
>#2. Don’t get high on your own supply
Saylor says “Never sell”, sells, plays with leverage as retarded as staking Kusama. BWAH HAHHAHAHA

62453045
>>62452752
AI will be transacting in bitcoin? Interesting times ahead.

62453084
>>62450537
He could have also borrowed against his BTC on maybe Babylon, but he sold kek.

So much for the BTC diamond hands

62454374
>>62450537
this is super bullish my fellow goyim.

62442771

62443188
>>62442771

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62443215
>>62442769

>>62443188

62443228
>>62442769

62443327
>>62442764
we shouldn't have listened to that chink.

62443363
>>62442764
We literally can't stop winning, it's not fair, not fair at all fellow SPCX chads

62443369
>>62442764

62443466
>>62442764
Don't worry, bobro, the market is VERY long. They are going to punish bulls.

62443506
>>62442764
>this weak ass euphoria pump
It will fail like all the others.

62443529
Are you all genuinely retarded? It's a three day trading weekend. When all markets are otherwise closed there is lower volume and the bots can wash trade the price up. Then Saylor can dump you next week.

62443719
EMERGENCY 63.2k IS VULNERABLE
MUMU HAS ASSUMED DIRECT CONTROL

62443731
>>62443529
Not all markets are from the usa.

62443786

62443790
do you bobo posters actually make short positions on btc every day?

62443819
>>62443790

62443826
>>62443808

62443854
easy bros
the $20k is programmed

62444157
>>62443731
That's why I called this a week ago and you're still trying to figure it out

62444160
>>62442764
July 4 pump and dump. Will return to normal on Monday. We got this bobos!

62447497
>>62443786
God, i hope so.

62447519
She’s gonna leave him, isn’t she?

62448917
t-the emergency is still going bobros, mumus are buying coins at any price

62448931

62450920
>>62442764
What 'emergency meeting'? Strategy just sold bitcoin.

62451017
>>62442769

62454504
